nl_proof: Explained:
Sentences used:
(1) Squirrels can fly.
(2) Which can fly?
Proof steps:
(1) A squirrel is a squirrel  [sentence 1]
(2) If some X is a squirrel then sk3 of X is a fly event  [sentence 1]
(3) sk3 of $some_squirrel is a fly event  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) If X is a fly event and some W performs X and X is an activity and X is a capability then W is the answer  [sentence 2]
(5) If the flying event sk3 of $some_squirrel is an activity and sk3 of $some_squirrel is a capability and some X3 performs sk3 of $some_squirrel then X3 is the answer  [from steps 3, 4]
(6) If some X is a squirrel then the flying event sk3 of X is an activity  [sentence 1]
(7) The flying event sk3 of $some_squirrel is an activity  [from steps 1, 6]
(8) If the flying event sk3 of $some_squirrel is a capability and some X performs sk3 of $some_squirrel then X is the answer  [from steps 5, 7]
(9) If some X is a squirrel then X performs the flying event sk3 of X  [sentence 1]
(10) A squirrel performs the flying event sk3 of $some_squirrel  [from steps 1, 9]
(11) If the flying event sk3 of $some_squirrel is a capability then a squirrel is the answer  [from steps 8, 10]
(12) If some X is a squirrel then normally the flying event sk3 of X is a capability  [sentence 1]
(13) Normally the flying event sk3 of $some_squirrel is a capability  [from steps 1, 12]
(14) A squirrel is the answer, except when the flying event sk3 of $some_squirrel is not a capability  [from steps 11, 13]
(15) technical answer unwrap step  [from question]
(16) A squirrel is an answer, except when the flying event sk3 of $some_squirrel is not a capability  [from steps 14, 15]
Exceptions checked and not holding:
the flying event sk3 of $some_squirrel is not a capability
